evidence.scientific_clarity: All brief requirements met: ~80-point scatter, OLS line, R²/slope/intercept annotated in-figure, OursModel point highlighted and labelled with arrow.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype=0 set anywhere in gen_figure.py; default DejaVu Sans used throughout — text will be Type-3 bitmap in the PDF, not selectable.
evidence.palette: Blue (#4878CF) / red (#E84040) / orange (#F5A623) is functional but not an explicit colorblind-safe palette (e.g. Okabe-Ito); red OLS line on blue scatter can be ambiguous for deuteranopes.
evidence.layout_density: figsize=(6,4.5) with tight_layout(); legend offset to (0.97,0.78) to clear annotation box at (0.97,0.97); grid, title, and axis labels all present without crowding.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content and includes numerical claims; however legend and annotation box share the upper-right corner and may overlap at some render sizes, and DejaVu Sans is non-standard for journals.
evidence.vector_fidelity: figure.pdf saved but pdf.fonttype is not set to 42, so fonts are embedded as Type 3 (non-selectable bitmaps) rather than TrueType; svg.fonttype also not set.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf, figure.png, figure.svg, caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial; exceeds minimum requirements.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states n=80, gives full OLS equation (y = -0.0449x + 0.621), R²=0.877, names OursModel with log10P≈9.45, and contextualises within Chinchilla-style power-law scaling.

overall_strengths: Complete implementation of all brief requirements (scatter, OLS fit, in-figure annotation, OursModel highlight) with a well-matched, numerically rich caption.
All required output files present (pdf, png, svg, caption.tex, gen_figure.py) and layout is clean with tight_layout and sensible panel proportions.
overall_weaknesses: Missing matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype'] = 42 and svg.fonttype = 0 — text in both PDF and SVG is non-selectable Type-3 bitmap, a hard rejection criterion at most journals.
Default DejaVu Sans font and non-colorblind-safe palette (no Okabe-Ito or similar); legend and annotation box both anchored to the upper-right corner risk overlap.
